title: Parametrize gate mode update tests in test_gates.py
analysis: Task 64 is a pure test-refactoring task touching only tests/test_gates.py. Two structurally identical tests — test_update_gate_mode (line 137, mode='pause') and test_update_gate_notify_mode (line 146, mode='notify') — follow the same pattern: PUT /api/gates/analyze with a mode, assert 200, assert returned mode matches, re-GET for persistence. The notify variant has an extra page-rendering check (lines 154-157), but test_toggle_shows_current_mode (line 323) already covers page rendering after mode changes with both positive and negative controls. The plan: (1) add a @pytest.mark.parametrize('mode', ['pause', 'notify']) test that unifies both, (2) remove the two old tests, (3) fix selected.get('analyze') -> selected['analyze'] on line 427 for dict-access consistency. Since this is test refactoring (no production code changes), TDD steps are: write new parametrized test (should pass immediately against existing API), remove old tests, fix the .get() call.

steps.description: Add parametrized test_update_gate_mode_persists(mode) to TestGateUpdateAPI with @pytest.mark.parametrize('mode', ['pause', 'notify']). The body: PUT /api/gates/analyze with json={'mode': mode}, assert response.status_code == 200, assert response.json()['mode'] == mode, re-GET /api/gates/analyze and assert check.json()['mode'] == mode. Keep both old tests temporarily so the new one coexists. Run pytest tests/test_gates.py to verify the new parametrized test passes for both parameter values alongside the old tests.

Remove the two old individual tests (test_update_gate_mode and test_update_gate_notify_mode) from TestGateUpdateAPI. The parametrized test from step-1 now fully replaces them. Run pytest tests/test_gates.py to verify no regressions — test count stays the same (2 old IDs removed, 2 parametrized IDs added).

In TestHtmxResponses.test_put_gate_returns_html_for_htmx, change selected.get('analyze') on line 427 to selected['analyze'] for consistency with all other dict accesses on the selected dict throughout the file. Run pytest tests/test_gates.py::TestHtmxResponses to confirm the fix doesn't break anything.

design_decisions.decision: Drop the page-rendering assertion (lines 154-157) from the parametrized test rather than including it for both modes
design_decisions.rationale: test_toggle_shows_current_mode (line 323) already covers page rendering after mode changes with both a positive assertion (analyze == 'pause') and a negative control (classify == 'auto'). Including page rendering in the update-persistence test would add redundant coverage and conflate two concerns: API persistence vs HTML rendering.

design_decisions.decision: Name the new test test_update_gate_mode_persists to clearly describe the combined behavior
design_decisions.rationale: The name communicates what's being tested (mode update + persistence via re-GET) and avoids collision with the old test_update_gate_mode name during the coexistence step (step-1).

design_decisions.decision: Use direct dict access selected['analyze'] instead of selected.get('analyze')
design_decisions.rationale: Every other dict access on `selected` in the file uses direct bracket notation (lines 331, 334, 473-475). Using .get() masks potential KeyError failures that would indicate a real test issue — the key should always exist if the page renders correctly.

reuse.how: Follow the same decorator style used in the project: @pytest.mark.parametrize('param_name', [values]) placed directly above the method definition

reuse.what: Existing PUT-assert-GET pattern from the two tests being merged
reuse.how: The new parametrized test body is the shared structure: client.put with mode, assert 200 + returned mode, re-GET for persistence
